Travel Guide

Gdańsk Weekend Getaway

Gdańsk, Poland · 17–18 July 2026

Summary Getting there Itinerary Stay Activities Dining Budget Weather Packing Tips

📋 Trip Summary

👪 2 adults 🚗 Car 🏰 Old Town🌊 Waterfront🍴 Local Food 💰 1,230 PLN

Destination: Gdańsk, Poland — a short, relaxed Old Town / waterfront getaway: evening arrival Friday, one full sightseeing day Saturday, evening drive home.

Dates: Depart Friday, 17 July 2026 (after 18:00) — return Saturday, 18 July 2026 (evening); 2 calendar days, 1 night.

Party: 2 adults

Transport mode: Car (own vehicle, round trip) — no rental needed for this same-car round trip.

Budget: 1,230 PLN against a limit of 1,300 PLN (UNDER by ~70 PLN (~5% margin))

Interests covered:
  • Old Town sightseeing: St. Mary's Basilica, Amber Museum, European Solidarity Centre, and evening/daytime walks through Długi Targ.
  • Waterfront: Motława River Embankment (Długie Pobrzeże) walks, day and night, with views of the Gdańsk Crane.
  • Local food: milk bars, pierogi, and Baltic-region dining across all four trip meals.

🚗 Getting There & Around

Round trip via the A1/S6 expressway between Łódź and Gdańsk.
Leg Detail Day/Date Duration Est. Cost
Outbound Łódź → Gdańsk via A1/S6 expressway (through Włocławek, Grudziądz), 339 km Fri, 17 Jul 2026 (departing after 18:00) ~3h 25min 183 PLN (fuel + toll)
Return Gdańsk → Łódź via the same A1/S6 route, 339 km Sat, 18 Jul 2026 (departing ~18:00–19:00) ~3h 25min 183 PLN (fuel + toll)
Parking Interparking Centrum Gdańsk (covered garage, steps from Old Town), ~20h Fri evening–Sat evening Fri 17 – Sat 18 Jul 2026 ~20 hours ≈60 PLN

Local transport

  • Old Town / waterfront is fully walkable once parked — no local transit needed for sightseeing. Interparking Centrum Gdańsk rates
  • Alternative for a short daytime stop: on-street Śródmieście paid parking zone (SPP), 7.50 PLN for the first hour, rising per hour. Gdańsk paid parking zones (gdansk.pl)
Transport subtotal used in budget: 427 PLN

🗓️ Day-by-Day Itinerary

Day 1 — Friday, 17 July 2026 — Łódź → Gdańsk

  • After 18:00Depart Łódź for Gdańsk via the A1/S6 expressway (through Włocławek, Grudziądz), 339 km, ~3h 25min. Fuel + toll ≈ 183 PLN.
  • ~21:30–22:00Arrival in Gdańsk; car parked at Interparking Centrum Gdańsk.
  • Check-inCheck in at Moon Hostel Gdańsk (private double room, private bath) — 24h reception comfortably supports the late arrival.
  • EveningEvening walk: Długi Targ (Long Market) & Neptune's Fountain, floodlit Old Town, 45–60 min.
  • EveningMotława River Embankment (Długie Pobrzeże) night stroll, views of the Crane, 30–45 min. Dress in layers — light rain risk ~13%.
  • DinnerPierogarnia Mandu (Centrum, ul. Elżbietańska 4/8), kitchen open to 22:00, ~45–55 PLN/person. Vegan/vegetarian pierogi options available. Backup if closed/full: Restauracja Kubicki (ul. Wartka 5), open to 23:00.

Day 2 — Saturday, 18 July 2026 — Gdańsk → Łódź

  • BreakfastPomelo Bistro Bar (ul. Ogarna 121/122), served 09:00–13:00, ≈45–60 PLN/person. Vegetarian egg/bread/cheese plates available.
  • MorningSt. Mary's Basilica (Bazylika Mariacka) — nave visit (free) + tower climb (409 steps, panoramic view), 1–1.5 hr. Tower: 20 PLN/adult × 2 = 40 PLN. Weather-flexible — do the climb if morning is dry.
  • Late morningAmber Museum (Muzeum Bursztynu), Gdańsk History Museum branch, 1–1.5 hr, 37 PLN/adult × 2 = 74 PLN. Indoor rain-contingency pick.
  • MiddayEuropean Solidarity Centre (Europejskie Centrum Solidarności), permanent exhibition, 1.5–2 hr, 40 PLN/adult × 2 = 80 PLN (audioguide included). Fully indoor, lift available.
  • LunchBar Mleczny Neptun (ul. Długa 33/34), classic milk bar, ~10:00–19:00 weekends, full lunch ≈25–35 PLN/person. Alternative: Pyra Bar (ul. Garbary 6/7), ≈20–40 PLN/person.
  • AfternoonMotława River Embankment (Długie Pobrzeże) daytime walk + Gdańsk Crane exterior view, 45–60 min, free. Best in a drier window — umbrella recommended (26% shower risk).
  • DinnerRestauracja Kubicki (ul. Wartka 5), open 12:00–23:00, fish/meat mains, ≈100–130 PLN/person for a starter + main. Budget alternative: Pierogarnia Mandu again, ~45–55 PLN/person.
  • ~18:00–19:00Depart Gdańsk for Łódź via the same A1/S6 route, 339 km, ~3h 25min. Fuel + toll ≈ 183 PLN.
Pacing note: doing all four paid Saturday activities plus a full lunch is a full, tightly-paced day given the evening departure — travelers preferring a lighter pace can drop either the Amber Museum or the European Solidarity Centre without affecting the rest of the plan.

🏠 Where You're Staying

Stop Property Stars Key Features Cost/night × Nights Subtotal
Gdańsk Moon Hostel Gdańsk (private double room, private bath) — 24h staffed reception; free WiFi; guest kitchen access; private bathroom; ~2 min walk to the Green Gate and Old Town (ul. Długie Ogrody 6A) 119 PLN × 1 night 119 PLN
Accommodation total ~179 PLN (incl. Interparking fallback)
Check-in/out: 24h staffed reception supports the ~21:30 Friday arrival; no published checkout hour was found — reconfirm directly with the property (+48 500 331 110) ahead of the Saturday evening departure.
Parking: on-site parking exists but must be pre-booked by phone and is unpriced — this plan instead uses the Interparking Centrum Gdańsk garage fallback (see Getting There & Around).
Note on source discrepancy: an earlier accommodation option (ibis Gdańsk Stare Miasto, 241 PLN/night) was considered but replaced by Moon Hostel in the latest accommodation revision to keep the trip under the 1,300 PLN cap. Moon Hostel is the property reflected in this plan and in the budget total.

Local food to try

Pierogi (Pierogarnia Mandu), classic milk-bar fare (Bar Mleczny Neptun, Pyra Bar), and fish/meat mains at Restauracja Kubicki — every recommended venue has at least one vegetarian option.
Food subtotal used in budget: 430 PLN

💰 Budget Summary

Category Notes Cost
Transport Fuel, toll, parking 427 PLN
Accommodation Moon Hostel + Interparking fallback ~179 PLN
Activities Saturday paid attractions, excl. lunch 194 PLN
Food 2 adults, low-cost scenario across 4 meals 430 PLN
Subtotal 1,230 PLN
Contingency 5% buffer ~62 PLN
Total with contingency ~1,292 PLN
Estimated total 1,230 PLN
Against the limit: PASS — 70 PLN under the 1,300 PLN cap without contingency (5.4% margin); ~8 PLN under cap with the 5% contingency buffer applied (0.6% margin).

🌤️ Weather

Mild, partly cloudy conditions both days with a low-to-moderate chance of light rain; pack a light jacket and umbrella, and keep a flexible indoor option for Saturday's showers.

Day/Date Conditions High / Low Precip. chance
Fri, 17 Jul (evening/night) ⛅ Partly cloudy to overcast, light–moderate wind 22.9°C / 20.5°C 13% (1.2 mm/day)
Sat, 18 Jul (day) ⛅ Partly cloudy, scattered showers possible, UV up to 4.7 24.7°C / 17.6°C 26% (4.05 mm)
Saturday's 26% shower chance means the Amber Museum and European Solidarity Centre double as rain contingencies; do the St. Mary's tower climb and outdoor waterfront walks in drier windows (morning/early evening).

💡 Travel Tips

  • Currency: PLN — ATMs widely available in Gdańsk; keep some small cash for parking meters/SPP zone.
  • No visa/ID concerns: domestic Poland trip — ID cards suffice, no passport needed.
  • Dietary: no dietary constraints stated by travelers; every recommended venue nonetheless has at least one vegetarian option.
  • Accessibility: no accessibility needs stated; note the St. Mary's tower climb (409 steps, narrow spiral stairs) is strenuous and not accessibility-friendly — the nave (free) is a step-free alternative, and the Amber Museum and European Solidarity Centre serve as accessible, fully indoor options (ECS has a lift).
  • Weather flexibility: Saturday's 26% shower chance means the Amber Museum and European Solidarity Centre double as rain contingencies; do the St. Mary's tower climb and outdoor waterfront walks in drier windows (morning/early evening).
  • Pacing: doing all four paid Saturday activities plus a full lunch is a full, tightly-paced day given the evening departure — travelers preferring a lighter pace can drop either the Amber Museum or the European Solidarity Centre without affecting the rest of the plan.
  • Parking confirmation: Moon Hostel's own on-site parking must be pre-booked by phone (+48 500 331 110) and its price is unpublished; this plan defaults to the Interparking Centrum Gdańsk fallback already reflected in the budget above.
